summary:
Iron deficiency anemia is indeed a common complication of chronic kidney disease, affecting up to 90% of dialysis-dependent patients and driven by factors such as recurrent blood loss during dialysis, impaired iron absorption, and increased iron demand from erythropoietin-stimulating agents. Guidelines consistently support IV iron supplementation when indicated, confirming its central role in managing CKD-associated anemia.  

Final Answer: \boxed{A}

Guidelines consistently support IV iron supplementation when indicated, confirming its central role in managing CKD-associated anemia.  

Final Answer: \boxed{A}. Answer choice: A
